seo.title: Building Next.js App Router patterns with Vercel and Clou...
seo.description: A step-by-step implementation guide for Next.js App Router patterns with Vercel and Cloudflare Pages.

content.intro: Integrate AI SDKs into Next.js applications while securing API keys and managing rate limits through server-side handling and middleware.
prerequisites: AI API key from provider (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic)

steps.title: Configure environment variables
steps.explanation: Store AI API keys in .env.local with NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix for client access and NEXT_PRIVATE_ for server-only use.

steps.title: Create server-side API route
steps.explanation: Implement a protected API route to handle AI SDK requests, preventing direct client-side key exposure.
pitfalls: Exposing NEXT_PRIVATE_ variables in client-side code

steps.title: Implement rate limiting middleware
steps.explanation: Add middleware to restrict API call frequency per user session using Redis or in-memory tracking.

steps.title: Build client-side interaction component
steps.explanation: Create a React component that sends user input to the protected API route with proper error handling.

steps.title: Set up logging and monitoring
steps.explanation: Integrate with Vercel's logging or external services to track API usage patterns and detect anomalies.

code.content:
Vercel CLI: vercel logs --type=error
Third-party: Sentry or Datadog integration

content.conclusion: This implementation secures AI API keys through server-side handling, prevents rate abuse with middleware, and provides structured client interactions while maintaining compliance with deployment constraints.
